_comment: Illustrative LLM output. Actual scores will vary.

result.paydown_recommendation: With 8 story points of sprint capacity, prioritise TD-003 (5pt) as the single highest-risk item — it can cause a full database connection pool exhaustion that would take down the entire API. Use the remaining 3 points to begin TD-001 (replace bcrypt.compareSync with async), which is the second-highest blast radius item and its risk is increasing with traffic growth.

scored_items.rationale: 22 months old, no workaround, and directly risks database connection pool exhaustion. This is the most dangerous item: a slow query on any of the three unprotected routes could make the entire API unresponsive. Risk trajectory is increasing as traffic grows.

Synchronous bcrypt in the event loop is a latency bomb under concurrent load. The current rate-limit workaround reduces — but does not eliminate — the risk. As the user base grows, the probability of a concurrent login spike increases.

High velocity tax: every developer working on the task list must work around the re-render behaviour. Blast radius is medium — it degrades UX but does not cause failures. Memoisation refactor is a well-understood fix — schedule for next quarter.
Minimal blast radius, no functional risk, no velocity tax. Configure PurgeCSS as part of a build system modernisation effort, not as a standalone sprint item.
